D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

Hot Foil Stamping

a heated die transfers a metallic or holographic skin onto paper — holographic foil already shows many hues, and tilting slides them across the surface

Hot foil stamping presses a heated metal die against a thin foil ribbon; the heat releases the metallised or holographic layer onto the paper exactly where the die touches. Plain gold foil reflects like solid metal — only the specular highlight slides as you tilt. Holographic foil is a surface-relief diffraction grating, so different positions split white light into different hues at the same time.

Tilt the stamped logo and watch what travels: on gold foil, a single bright sheen slides; on holographic foil, the whole rainbow shifts. Even at zero tilt the holographic foil already shows multiple colours across its surface — that is the diffraction grating, not a colour shift.
